title: Streptavidin-CBD Purification
author: Manuela De Las Casas
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6e8hbhw

steps.title: From the Petri dishes with the...
steps.instruction: From the Petri dishes with the bacteria, pick a few colonies with a Digralsky planting handle and resuspend the colonies in liquid LB in an Erlenmeyer flask. It is recommended to set more than one flask, to minimize risks in case one of the flasks goes wrong. Grow a negative control as well (the bacteria without the plasmid).

steps.title: Let the bacteria grow in an...
steps.instruction: Let the bacteria grow in an incubator at 37ºC until it reaches an OD600 of 0.6 approximately (range 0.4-0.8). Check periodically the OD with the ultrospec. To check the OD, set a blank cuvette with just LB to compare the bacteria grow. Be careful not to overgrow the bacteria. If overgrown, they won't be useful for transformation. The culture should be at log phase. The volume pipetted into the cuvettes is discarded after the measurements. Do not return it to the flask.

steps.title: Since the promoter used for this...
steps.instruction: Since the promoter used for this bacteria has a constitutive expression, there is no need to induce. But depending on the promoter you used, you might need to induce the expression of the protein. In our case, we just need to let the bacteria grow as step 1.1 indicates.

steps.title: To purifiy the protein, it is...
steps.instruction: To purifiy the protein, it is necessary to lysate the cells. In order to do so:

steps.title: Pick 1-2 mL of bacteria, centrifugate...
steps.instruction: Pick 1-2 mL of bacteria, centrifugate 3-5 min at 15000 x g. After centrifugation, take out the supernatant carefully and resuspend the cells pellet with B-mercaptoethanol and SDS.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL. This step is not necessary to obtain the protein. But with the purified protein, it is recommended to check that the procedure has gone right and that we have the desired protein. Boil the samples 3-5 min at 98ºC-100ºC. After boiling, centrifugate again for 3-5 min at 15000 x g. Run an electrophoresis (follow an electrophoresis protocol). Recommended to use 1:10 and 1:100 dilutions from the supernatant in an SDS-page acrylamide gel (pore size 10-12%). Materials and steps for the electrophoresis are not contemplated in this protocol.
